properties.Test Case: make sure the results are expected: missing image descriptions should generate WCAG 2.0 A violations, but everything else should be non-critical. (Expected: Minor, Best Practice issues "aria-allowed-role", and Moderate Best Practice "landmark-unique" rule.) And there are more violations we are "living with" in BL-11704.
properties.Steps: - make sure the results are expected: missing image descriptions should generate WCAG 2.0 A violations, but everything else should be non-critical. properties.Test Case: ensure image description appear below images [requires flowable mode]
properties.Steps: - ensure image description appear below images [requires flowable mode]

properties.Test Case: Test changing playback ordering including Image Descriptions [Advanced section of Talking Book Tool]. By default, things play back in dom order.
properties.Steps: - Test changing playback ordering including Image Descriptions [Advanced section of Talking Book Tool]. By default, things play back in dom order.

properties.Test Case: Test Book Metadata. Fill in the blanks. Check resulting ePUB in Daisy Simply Reading or Readium or eKitabu (on Windows) and see if author shows up. Save the file to disk, rename it as a zip file. Unzip it and examine contents. Under the folder "content", there is a file called "content.opf". Rename it to .xml and open with an xml editor such as XMLMind Edit (https://www.xmlmind.com/xmleditor/). Ensure all the Metadata info has been transferred.
properties.Steps: - Test Book Metadata. Fill in the blanks. Check resulting ePUB in Daisy Simply Reading or Readium or eKitabu (on Windows) and see if author shows up. Save the file to disk, rename it as a zip file. Unzip it and examine contents. Under the folder "content", there is a file called "content.opf". Rename it to .xml and open with an xml editor such as XMLMind Edit (https://www.xmlmind.com/xmleditor/). Ensure all the Metadata info has been transferred.

properties.Test Case: With file meddler running, make a new book and paste in a large image to the front cover. Return to Collections tab. Repeat test, this time giving the book a title.
